Finding: grounding opened the binding gate; the ceiling is the task family

The grounded ladder took binding from chance to about 0.78 at fixed model size, opening the M1 gate decisively, but binding plateaus from the micro rung up, finetuning adds nothing because co-training saturates the same pair distribution, and the model fabricates in perfect form whenever facts are absent, so the next lever is richer generation, not more scale.

Rung results (pretrain/sft binding): pico 0.28/0.25, nano 0.56/0.56, micro 0.78/0.81, full-mini 0.75/0.78, with fluency rising monotonically to coherence 7 at full. The micro-to-full step spent 2.4x parameters and 2x data for zero binding gain, so the 0.8 ceiling is a property of the task distribution, not capacity. Pair perplexity is already near 1.5 at pretrain by micro, so finetuning early-stops with nothing left to teach. Asked anything whose facts are not in context, the model samples a plausible fact-world and answers in perfect trained form.

This is the M1 milestone (node 48) resolved, and it confirms the central bet of grounded generation (node 46) against the scale-only null result (node 47): at identical size, coherent data moved binding 26x past the gate while parameters alone had moved it not at all; even the 0.43M pico clears the old floor. Three secondary findings redirect the plan. First, the plateau answers M2's axis question (node 49) with neither pre-committed axis: more same-kind data and more parameters both saturate, so the return is in harder and broader task families. Second, SFT flatness is a distribution property, not a recipe failure: the co-trained instruction stream already teaches the entire pair distribution, so a meaningful pretrain-to-sft delta requires reserving capabilities for finetuning (node 52). Third, the model has no epistemic boundary: no training pair ever showed facts being absent, so it cannot decline and instead confabulates in form, which interactive probing confirmed along with unstable self-generated names and zero coverage of degenerate logic. A separate cost surfaced: worldgen's single artisan vocabulary reintroduced the diversity contraction concern (node 35) from the program side, and the misaligned real-world evaluation sets initially misread the whole run as a regression, motivating the matched-evaluation principle in node 52.
